headlineSummary: Sentiment was most favorable toward Anthropic and Google Gemini, mixed on OpenAI, and negative on Microsoft Copilot, with limited evidence behind Copilot and Gemini [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot was negative, driven by a comment describing Microsoft and GitHub as pushing Copilot on users for years [E2].
OpenAI was mixed to neutral overall: one comment praised Codex as shockingly good, while another criticized it as slow and messy and said OpenAI is doomed [E1][E3].
Google Gemini had a positive but limited-evidence signal from a commenter who said Gemini 3 flash seemed smarter, faster to iterate with, and had better common sense [E1].
Anthropic drew positive reactions: one commenter said Claude Code handled the same coding tasks quickly and successfully, and another credited Anthropic with building a tool that took off [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: I would rather YOLO code with Gemini 3 flash, since it's actually smarter in my assessment, and at least I can iterate faster, and it feels like it has better common sense. Just as an example, I found an old, terrible app I made years ago for our firm that handles room reservations. I told it to update from Bootstrap to Flowbite UI. Codex just took forever to make a mess, installed version 2.7 when 4.0.1 is the latest, even when I explicitly stated that it should use the absolute latest version. Then it tried to install it and failed, so it reverted to the outdated CDN. I gave the same task to Claude Code. Same prompt. It one-shotted it quickly. Then I asked it to swap out ALL the fetch logic to have SPA-like functionality with the new beta 4 version of HTMX, and it one-shot that too in the time Codex spent just trying to read a few files in the project. This reminds me of the feeling I had when I got the Nokia N800. It was so promising on paper, but the product was so bad and terrible to use that I knew Nokia was done for. If this was their take on what an acceptable smartphone could be, it proves that the whole foundation is doomed. If this is OpenAI's take on what an agentic coding assistant should be—something that can run by itself and iterate until it completes its task in an intelligent and creative way.... OpenAI is doomed.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Gemini 3 flash is smarter, faster to iterate with, and has better common sense than Codex.
The commenter says Claude Code completed the same tasks quickly and successfully in one shot, strongly favoring it on coding performance.
The commenter describes Codex as slow and messy and concludes with 'OpenAI is doomed,' making this strong negative evidence about OpenAI's coding assistant quality.

evidence.excerpt: It really says something that MS/Github has been trying to shovel Copilot down our throats for years, and Anthropic just builds a tool in a short period of time and it takes off.

annotations.rationale: The commenter characterizes Microsoft's promotion of Copilot as 'trying to shovel Copilot down our throats,' a strongly negative comparison.
The commenter credits Anthropic with building a tool that 'takes off,' expressing approval relative to Copilot.

evidence.excerpt: Genuinely excited to try this out. I've started using Codex much more heavily in the past two months and honestly, it's been shockingly good. Not perfect mind you, but it keeps impressing me with what it's able to "get". It often gets stuff wrong, and at times runs with faulty assumptions, but overall it's no worse than having average L3-L4 engs at your disposal.

annotations.rationale: In the Codex App story on openai.com, the commenter says Codex has been 'shockingly good' and keeps impressing them, despite some caveats.
